title: Just for You - Educators - Canada.ca
content:
## Just for You - Educators
The Government of Canada publishes many teaching resources and kits that can be used by educators to promote good health practices.
For a list of resources about your health and the surrounding environment, food safety and consumer product safety, consult the Consumer Information page.
- A Resource Guide on Family Violence Issues for Aboriginal Communities
- Eating Well with Canada's Food Guide - First Nations, Inuit and Métis
- Inside the Laboratory
- Your Child's Prescription: Important Questions to Ask
- Canadian Guidelines for Sexual Health Education
- National Children's Agenda
- Trends in the Health of Canadian Youth Report on the health behaviours and attitudes of young people.
- Canada's food guide resources
- Canada's Physical Activity Guide for Children and Youth
- Teacher's Guide to Physical Activity for Children
- Teacher's Guide to Physical Activity for Youth
- 'Gotta Move!' Interactive Magazine for Children
- 'Let's Get Active!' Interactive Magazine for Youth
- Canada's Physical Activity Guide for Children and Youth
- Mauve (CD-ROM made by and for youth)
- Mental Health - Coping with Stress It's Your Health
- Population Health Web Site
- Arts and Crafts Materials (It's Your Health)
- Sexual Health Promotion
Health Canada shares a joint initiative with Environment Canada called the UV Index Sun Awareness Program. This program is aimed at elementary and high school teachers to teach their students how to use the UV index as a tool to better protect themselves from the harmful UV rays from the sun. Consult our UV Index Sun Awareness Program section to learn more about the program, consult our resources and register your school.
- National Clearinghouse on Family Violence
- Reducing Work-Life Conflict: What Works? What Doesn't?
Our goal is to make this page as useful as possible and your comments can help.
